rationale: The cited paper is referenced multiple times in the body as more than a background list item. Gneezy uses Putnam-Farr and Riis (2016) to support specific methodological claims: that field experiments can capture downstream effects and improve communication effectiveness, and that real-world studies often require design compromises. The citing paper even points to concrete implementation issues reported in the cited paper's Experiment 2, indicating an explicit functional role in the argument.
relation_description: The citing article draws on Putnam-Farr and Riis (2016) as an illustrative empirical case supporting its methodological argument for field experiments in marketing. It uses that study both as evidence of field-experiment value in communication settings and as an example of practical design tradeoffs in applied field work.
